title: Alki Neighborhood Self Guided Tour - Southwest Seattle Historical Society - Log House Museum
content: [![Image 4: Logo for Southwest Seattle Historical Society - Log House Museum](https://loghousemuseum.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/cropped-SWSHS-Logo-Horizontal.png)](https://loghousemuseum.org/). The Alki Homestead serves as the bookends of the tour with the Alki Statue of Liberty, the Founders Monument, the Denny Home Site, Doc Maynard’s Home, and the Log House Museum as our other main spots. “Alki Homestead: This Place Matters” _Historic Seattle National Trust for Historic Preservation_ 9 June 2010 [http://historicseattle.org/alki-homestead-this-place-matters/](http://www.google.com/url?q=http%3A%2F%2Fhistoricseattle.org%2Falki-homestead-this-place-matters%2F&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNGvcWLv3e9Fwz4BYY38ksquihURNQ). “Alki Homestead to be Restored” _Historic Seattle_ 18 March 2015 [https://historicseattle.org/alki-homestead-to-be-restored](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Fhistoricseattle.org%2Falki-homestead-to-be-restored&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNEOmk08zRzI_F-Ta6NDj9iclpQi_A). The Native Americans came out shortly after the Denny party landed on Alki out of curiosity, including Seattle. It is through Fay that the Denny party was transported up to Alki and through Seattle that Doc Maynard came to work and live in Alki, later to create the Seattle settlement. “Alki Homestead to be Restored” _Historic Seattle_ 18 March 2015 [https://historicseattle.org/alki-homestead-to-be-restored](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Fhistoricseattle.org%2Falki-homestead-to-be-restored&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNEOmk08zRzI_F-Ta6NDj9iclpQi_A). [Audio 9](https://loghousemuseum.org/wp-content/uploads/2018/10/Alki-Homestead-Preservation.mp3?_=9). “Alki Homestead: This Place Matters” _Historic Seattle National Trust for Historic Preservation_ 9 June 2010 [http://historicseattle.org/alki-homestead-this-place-matters/](http://www.google.com/url?q=http%3A%2F%2Fhistoricseattle.org%2Falki-homestead-this-place-matters%2F&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNGvcWLv3e9Fwz4BYY38ksquihURNQ). “Alki Homestead to be Restored” _Historic Seattle_ 18 March 2015 [https://historicseattle.org/alki-homestead-to-be-restored](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Fhistoricseattle.org%2Falki-homestead-to-be-restored&sa=D&sntz=1&usg=AFQjCNEOmk08zRzI_F-Ta6NDj9iclpQi_A). Southwest Seattle Historical Society Programs will not be affected by the closure.[](https://loghousemuseum.org/alki-walking-tour).
